free_text: Nuclear structure of 170Tm from neutron-capture
and (d,p)-reaction measurements
The 169Tm target had dimensions 1 x 4 mm2 and
thickness 40 mu-g/cm2.
For the measurements with 12- and
26-MeV deuterons, a proportional chamber with cathode
strip readout and a scintillator were used to record
the spectrum. Several overlapping spectra, each about
700 keV wide, were taken.
For the spectra taken with
Ed=20.0 MeV, a large focal plane detector consisting
of one single-wire counter, one multiwire proportional
chamber, and a scintillator was used.
Intensities in mu-b/sr were obtained from the beam
current which was measured by dumping it into a
Faraday cup. A repelling grid was placed in front of
the cup to prevent electron loss.
Possible multiplet
Statistical error in the level. Level
energies are average values from the experimental
measurements.
Uncorrelated calibration errors of about
30% are not included in the listed intensities.
Experimental error
Data from the table VI
